triggers.name: Monday weekly performance review
triggers.message:
[SCHEDULED] Monday Weekly Review Session — Execute ALL steps sequentially:

STEP 1: WEEKLY PERFORMANCE REVIEW
- Total touchpoints (CRs, DMs, comments) vs target last week
- Month-to-date progress vs 50% overdelivery goal
- Connection acceptance rate
- DM response rate

STEP 2: PIPELINE HEALTH
- Lead_CRM stage movements (who moved forward, who's stuck)
- Leads going cold (5+ days no response)
- Any leads needing Antara's intervention → IMMEDIATELY email + Slack

STEP 3: FUNNEL INTELLIGENCE
- Update sprint-prep/funnel-intelligence.md with:
  - Profile patterns that respond (title, segment, company type)
  - Template effectiveness (which messages got replies)
  - Drop analysis (where leads stall, why)
  - Predictive signals for this week

STEP 4: STRATEGY RECOMMENDATIONS
- Segment prioritization for this week
- Template adjustments
- Volume target adjustments
- Post full review to Slack

triggers.name: Morning session: inbox → scout → prep → sprint
triggers.message:
[SCHEDULED] Morning Session — Execute ALL steps sequentially:

STEP 1: INBOX CHECK
- Spawn operator to check Antara's LinkedIn messaging (linkedin.com/messaging/)
- Report: new replies, new messages, connection acceptances, DMs unanswered 3-4+ days
- Check notifications for engagement on our posts/comments
- Do NOT reply — just report
- If any lead shows meeting interest or shares contact info → flag URGENT for immediate escalation (email kagrawal29@gmail.com + sharma.ack@gmail.com, post on Slack)

STEP 2: RESEARCH SCOUT (Mon/Wed/Fri only — skip on Tue/Thu)
- Job scout: LinkedIn Jobs for 'voiceover', 'dubbing', 'localization' in India
- Group scan: Check joined VO/dubbing groups for direct requirements
- Flag buying signals and direct requirements as URGENT

STEP 3: CATCH-UP CHECK
- Find last completed sprint date (check sprint-prep/screenshots/)
- Compare with spreadsheet plan for missed days
- If days were missed: calculate adjusted daily target for 50% overdelivery by month end

STEP 4: AUTO-PREP
- Read today's segment from spreadsheet
- Read ICP details + templates
- Calculate volume targets (adjusted for catch-up if needed)
- Dedup against Lead_CRM
- Build execution sheet at sprint-prep/YYYY-MM-DD-segment.md

STEP 5: SELF-REVIEW
- No placeholders — all names, URLs, messages are real
- Actual posts for comments — verified current LinkedIn posts
- Personalized messages — beyond just company name
- Dedup check — no re-touching pipeline leads
- Volume check — within LinkedIn safe limits
- Fix any issues. Mark READY if all pass.

STEP 6: AUTO-SPRINT
- Spawn operator with sprint instructions
- Execute all touchpoints
- Touchpoints that count: CRs, DMs, comments ONLY
- Profile visits, likes, follows are supporting actions

STEP 7: POST-SPRINT SUMMARY
- Summarize results: touchpoints (CRs, DMs, comments) + supporting actions
- Update Lead_CRM with new touches + stage movements
- Update tracker sheet actuals
- Update funnel intelligence with response patterns
- Check month-to-date vs 50% overdelivery goal
- Post morning summary to Slack (ONE message covering all steps)

triggers.name: Evening session: reconcile → lead pulse → escalate
triggers.message:
[SCHEDULED] Evening Session — Execute ALL steps sequentially:

STEP 1: SPRINT RECONCILIATION
- Verify today's sprint actuals are in the spreadsheet
- If not updated during morning session, update now

STEP 2: LEAD PULSE
- Review all active leads in Lead_CRM
- Check for stage movements since morning
- Update Days_Since_Last_Touch
- Flag leads going cold (5+ days no response)

STEP 3: FUNNEL INTELLIGENCE UPDATE
- Record any new patterns from today's activities
- Note which profiles/templates performed

STEP 4: ESCALATION CHECK
- Any leads needing Antara's intervention?
- If yes: IMMEDIATELY email kagrawal29@gmail.com (TO) + sharma.ack@gmail.com (CC)
- Post same update on Slack

STEP 5: EVENING SUMMARY
- Post to Slack: today's results + pipeline movements + escalations + month-to-date progress

triggers.name: Friday next week plan
triggers.message: [SCHEDULED] Friday Review — Review next week's outreach plan. For each day: segment, volume targets, any prep needed. Flag days without prep sheets. Compare this week's performance. Suggest strategy adjustments. Post to Slack.

triggers.name: Biweekly email report
triggers.message: [SCHEDULED] Biweekly email — Send narrative-style email to kagrawal29@gmail.com (TO) and sharma.ack@gmail.com (CC) via Gmail MCP. Lead with insights/story, not status dump. Include: touchpoints, pipeline movements, hot leads, what's working, strategy adjustments, tracker link, upcoming plan.

triggers.name: Daily git push
triggers.message: [SCHEDULED] Daily git push — Stage all changes, write detailed commit message summarizing the day's work, push to origin/main.
